 {"id":"bd-ncivz.5","title":"E2E test: crash recovery with parallel WAL multi-buffer replay","description":"End-to-end test validating crash recovery correctness when using the parallel WAL with per-core buffers. Parallel WAL recovery is more complex than single-WAL recovery because log records are distributed across multiple buffers and must be replayed in epoch order.

## Test Scenarios

1. Crash mid-epoch: Kill process during an active epoch (some per-core buffers flushed, others not). Expected: recovery replays all committed transactions from flushed buffers. Unflushed committed transactions may be lost (within the documented durability window).

2. Crash during epoch flush: Kill process while the group commit flusher is writing per-core buffers to disk. Expected: partial flush produces a valid WAL (each buffer flush is atomic via aligned writes).

3. Crash with mixed epoch records: Multiple epochs in the WAL (some fully flushed, latest partially flushed). Expected: all fully-flushed epochs recovered completely.

4. Crash with concurrent SSI aborts: Kill process while SSI abort is rolling back writes. Expected: recovery correctly identifies and discards uncommitted (aborted) transactions.

5. Multi-buffer ordering correctness: After recovery, verify that the recovered state matches what would result from serial replay of all committed transactions in epoch order.

## Alien CS Reference
Tu et al. 2013 Silo: Section 4.2 describes epoch-based recovery protocol for parallel logging. Zheng et al. 2014: recovery ordering guarantees for distributed log buffers. Johnson et al. 2010 Aether: group commit crash recovery semantics and partial-flush handling.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Use the fail crate for crash injection at specific code points in the WAL flush path
- Implement crash scenarios as integration tests with #[test] and fork: use std::process::Command to spawn child process, then kill with SIGKILL at injection point
- Use tempfile::TempDir for isolated test databases (cleanup on drop)
- Recovery verification: compute BLAKE3 checksum of database state post-recovery, compare against expected state from known committed transactions
- Use crossbeam::scope for concurrent writer threads in test setup

## Logging Requirements
- Log every crash injection point and the state at crash time at tracing::info level
- Log recovery replay decisions: which epochs replayed, which transactions recovered/discarded at tracing::info level
- Log final verification result with expected vs actual checksums at tracing::info level
- Log partial-flush detection and handling at tracing::warn level
- Log any data loss events (unflushed committed transactions) at tracing::warn level

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 crash scenarios implemented and passing
- [ ] Recovery produces consistent database state in all scenarios
- [ ] No committed data lost (within documented durability window)
- [ ] No uncommitted data visible after recovery
- [ ] Multi-buffer replay ordering verified deterministic
- [ ] 50 randomized crash points per scenario
- [ ] CI integration (nightly, due to time cost)","acceptance_criteria":"1. End-to-end scripts cover happy-path and adversarial-path behavior with deterministic seeds, one-command replay, and explicit oracle checks. 2. Companion unit tests validate scenario generators, checkers, and artifact validators to prevent false positives in e2e outcomes. 3. Structured logs include trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, resource stats, and root-cause context sufficient for post-failure triage without re-instrumentation. 4. CI archives artifact bundles (logs, manifests, checksums) and fails on nondeterminism, missing artifacts, or schema violations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaDesert","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:26.587680794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T11:07:33.186986038Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T11:07:33.186911027Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1967,"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Evidence: 16/16 harness integration tests pass (crash_scenario_catalog_completeness, crash_scenario_fault_category_mapping, recovery_action_for_checksum_failure_mapping, wal_chain_invalid_reason_variants, wal_recovery_metrics_accumulation, fault_injection_spec_construction, fault_state_lifecycle_and_metrics, fault_metrics_snapshot_structure, group_commit_epoch_ordering_across_flush_cycles, cross_process_crash_roles_and_points, durability_matrix_crash_modes_and_lanes, compaction_policy_and_phase_machine, ssi_evidence_metrics_for_abort_tracking, recovery_decision_from_checksum_mismatch, multi_epoch_consolidator_with_interleaved_batches, conformance_summary). 6/6 conformance gates pass. CI gate: scripts/verify_bd_ncivz_5_crash_recovery_parallel_wal.sh. No warnings. Agent: MagentaDesert.","created_at":"2026-02-21T11:07:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ncivz.6","title":"Unit tests: per-core WAL buffer correctness and epoch ordering","description":"Unit tests for per-core WAL buffer correctness and epoch-based ordering guarantees. These tests validate the foundational mechanisms of parallel WAL before integration testing (bd-ncivz.5).

## Test Categories

1. Per-core buffer isolation: Verify that writes from different threads go to different buffers with no cross-contamination. Spawn N threads, each writing unique log records, verify each buffer contains only its thread records.

2. Epoch tagging correctness: Verify that log records are tagged with the correct epoch. Write records across an epoch boundary, verify records before the boundary have epoch E and records after have epoch E+1.

3. Buffer overflow handling: Fill a per-core buffer to capacity, verify overflow handling (either blocking or overflow buffer allocation) works correctly without data loss.

4. Double-buffering: Verify that write-to-A while flush-B works correctly. Simulate concurrent write and flush, verify no data corruption or loss.

5. Epoch advance fence: Verify that the epoch advance fence correctly separates records. All active transactions must observe the new epoch before any new records are tagged with it.

6. Sealed record integrity: Verify that sealed (committed) records cannot be modified. After transaction commit, the corresponding log records must be immutable.

7. Abort cleanup: Verify that aborted transaction log records are correctly marked as discarded and not replayed during recovery.

## Alien CS Reference
Tu et al. 2013 Silo: Section 4.1 specifies the per-core logging invariants and epoch fence protocol. The double-buffering scheme follows standard WAL buffer management from Mohan et al. 1992 ARIES.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Use std::thread::scope for structured concurrency in multi-threaded tests
- Verify buffer isolation with unique per-thread markers (thread::current().id() or custom u64)
- For epoch boundary tests: use std::sync::Barrier to synchronize threads at epoch advance points
- For double-buffering: use crossbeam::channel to simulate concurrent write/flush timing
- Use proptest to generate random write/commit/abort sequences for unit test coverage
- Each test should run in < 1 second (unit test performance requirement)

## Logging Requirements
- Log test setup at tracing::info level: test_name, num_threads, buffer_size
- Log epoch transitions observed during tests at tracing::debug level
- Log assertion details on failure at tracing::error level: expected_epoch, actual_epoch, record_details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 test categories implemented and passing
- [ ] Multi-threaded tests use structured concurrency (no detached threads)
- [ ] Buffer isolation verified under 8+ concurrent writers
- [ ] Epoch boundary correctness verified with barrier synchronization
- [ ] No data loss under any tested scenario
- [ ] All tests complete in < 5 seconds total
- [ ] Integrated into CI test suite (cargo test)","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit/property test matrix covers core invariants, edge cases, regression seeds, and negative-path behavior with deterministic fixtures. 2. At least one integrated end-to-end scenario validates the same capability in real file-backed execution and checks externally observable behavior. 3. Tests assert required structured logging/spans/metrics fields and causal traceability so failures are diagnosable without guesswork. 4. CI enforces deterministic outcomes, captures rich artifacts, and blocks merge on flake policy violations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CreamRaven","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:34:08.273765239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T16:14:02.193434227Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T16:13:09.679556741Z","close_reason":"Implemented missing per-core WAL unit-test coverage for sealed-lane immutability and abort cleanup; focused WAL tests and workspace check pass (fmt/clippy blocked by unrelated pre-existing workspace debt)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","durability","ev-gated","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1968,"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.6","author":"CreamRaven","text":"Implemented targeted closure for missing unit-test coverage in crates/fsqlite-wal/src/per_core_buffer.rs.

Changes:
- Added sealed-lane immutability test (bd_ncivz_1_sealed_lane_rejects_mutating_appends) to assert mutating appends are blocked once lane is sealed and commit metadata remains unchanged through flush handoff.
- Added abort-cleanup test (bd_ncivz_2_abort_cleanup_drops_non_committed_records) to ensure non-committed records (end_seq == None) are pruned before durable replay/recovery ordering.
- Added flush-lane cleanup step to drop non-committed records before epoch-straddle validation and replay ordering.
- Updated synthetic record helper to model committed records by default (end_seq = Some(...)) so abort cases are explicit and deterministic.

Validation (via rch):
1. rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-wal per_core_buffer -- --nocapture => PASS (14 tests)
2. rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets => PASS
3. rch exec -- cargo fmt --check => FAIL due pre-existing unrelated formatting diffs outside this bead
4. rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings => FAIL due pre-existing unrelated too_many_lines lints in fsqlite-mvcc

Bead-scope unit invariants are now covered and passing. Downstream e2e lane remains bd-ncivz.5.

 {"id":"bd-ncivz.7","title":"Alien Contract: Parallel WAL group-commit with deterministic fallback","description":"Change: Operationalize Parallel WAL (§15.4) with explicit two-barrier durability contract, deterministic ordering proof, and safe fallback to single WAL path.\n\nHotspot evidence: P0 bd-14m.2 + P2 bd-ncivz.1-.6 indicate write-path serialization and checkpoint debt as major throughput/tail bottlenecks.\n\nMapped graveyard sections: §15.4 Parallel WAL, §0.13 decision contract, §0.15 tail decomposition, §0.25 composability/interference matrix.\n\nEV score: (5*4*4)/(3*3)=8.89\nPriority tier: A\nAdoption wedge: shadow-write mode first (single WAL authoritative), compare proofs/checksums before enabling multi-buffer commit path.\nBudgeted mode: cap in-flight WAL buffers + group size + flush interval; on budget exhaustion force synchronous single-buffer flush.\nExpected-loss model: actions={parallel,single}; states={safe,ordering-risk,io-stall}; loss prioritizes ordering correctness over throughput gain.\nCalibration + fallback trigger: replay parity mismatch, durability witness gap, or p99 spike >20% for 3 windows triggers automatic fallback to single WAL.\nIsomorphism proof plan: commit visibility order equals marker order in both modes; frame content and checkpoint results byte-equivalent.\np50/p95/p99 target: +30/+50/+70% write throughput with p99 non-regression guard.\nPrimary risk + countermeasure: ordering bugs across buffers -> epoch sequencer + deterministic replay + fault injection matrix.\nRepro artifact pack: env/manifest/replay-lock/fault seeds/evidence ledger links.\nPrimary paper status: Silo/Aether references checklist required.\nInterference test: required with checkpoint scheduler and GC tuning controllers.\nDemo linkage: concurrent-writer scaling demo vs SQLite baseline.\nRollback: toggle to single WAL path; preserve parallel path artifacts for postmortem.\nBaseline comparator: existing single WAL append path.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1.  {"id":"bd-nlcyx","title":"Database Cracking / Adaptive Indexing (§8.8)","description":"Implement database cracking (Idreos et al. 2007) for adaptive, zero-admin indexing. Database cracking incrementally sorts data in response to queries, so each query partially sorts the data in the regions it accesses and frequently queried ranges become progressively faster.

## Mathematical Foundation
After Q queries on a column, the column is partitioned into O(Q) sorted segments. Lookup cost decreases from O(N) to O(N/Q) as more queries crack the data. In the limit, the column becomes fully sorted (equivalent to a sorted index) but without any upfront sort cost.

## Application in FrankenSQLite
- Auto-indexing for ad-hoc analytics (no CREATE INDEX needed)
- Columnar storage cracking for vectorized scans
- Adaptive to actual query patterns (indexes emerge where needed)

## Alien CS Reference
Idreos, Kersten, Manegold 2007 Database Cracking: original paper with amortized cost analysis. Idreos et al. 2011 Merging What is Cracked, Cracking What is Merged Adaptive Indexing in Main-Memory Column-Stores: adaptive merging optimization that reduces worst-case crack overhead. Halim et al. 2012 Stochastic Database Cracking: probabilistic analysis of cracking convergence under different query distributions.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Implement CrackedColumn<T: Ord> wrapping a Vec<T> with a BTreeMap<T, usize> crack index
- Crack operation: on range query [lo, hi], partition the column at lo and hi using std::slice::partition_point
- Crack index: BTreeMap mapping crack boundaries to positions in the underlying Vec
- For vectorized integration: crack operates on columnar batches, producing selection vectors for cracked ranges
- Use std::slice::select_nth_unstable for efficient in-place partitioning (O(n) expected)

## Logging Requirements
- Log crack operations at tracing::debug level: column_name, crack_key, partition_size
- Log crack index growth at tracing::info level periodically: num_cracks, avg_segment_size
- Log full-sort convergence at tracing::info level when column becomes fully sorted

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cracking prototype for single-column range queries
- [ ] Progressive refinement: repeated queries on same range get faster
- [ ] Benchmark: cracking vs explicit index vs full scan
- [ ] Integration with vectorized scan operator
- [ ] Correctness: cracked data produces same results as uncracked data","acceptance_criteria":"1. Implementation is complete and behaviorally correct against explicit invariants and edge-case requirements, including fault and recovery paths where applicable. 2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover normal, boundary, and adversarial conditions with deterministic fixtures and regression seeds. 3. End-to-end scenario scripts validate integrated behavior in real execution flows and include deterministic replay instructions. 4. Structured logging/metrics (traceable IDs, timing, counters, error context) are emitted and validated so failures are diagnosable and auditable.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:01.910628806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T07:33:27.206087753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T07:33:27.205993737Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adaptive","alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nlcyx","depends_on_id":"bd-14vp7.2","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1972,"issue_id":"bd-nlcyx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Evidence: CrackedColumn<T> implementation + 10/10 harness tests pass.

 {"id":"bd-nsvud","title":"TM: Track M unit + e2e tests — value slab allocator correctness, zero malloc in steady state","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:49:01.912520810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:27:07.674370984Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nsvud","depends_on_id":"bd-wi4w4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:49:02.452116362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3404,"issue_id":"bd-nsvud","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Unit Tests (crates/fsqlite-types/src/value.rs)
1. test_slab_basic_alloc_dealloc — allocate and return 100 values, verify slab reuse
2. test_slab_exhaustion_fallback — exhaust 256 slots, verify fallback to global allocator
3. test_slab_no_leak — create+drop 10K values, verify slab size stays bounded
4. test_slab_thread_local_isolation — values allocated on thread A don't appear on thread B's slab
5. test_slab_zero_malloc_steady_state — after warmup (256 allocs), next 1000 allocs use zero malloc (counting allocator)

## E2E Tests
6. test_slab_insert_10k — 10K row INSERT, verify no value corruption, oracle comparison
7. test_slab_mixed_types — INSERT rows with INTEGER, TEXT, BLOB, NULL columns, verify correctness

## Logging
- slab_alloc_count, slab_return_count, global_alloc_fallback_count, slab_high_water_mark per test

 {"id":"bd-numl","title":"§4.19.1-4.19.5 Remote Effects Contract: RemoteCap + Named Computations + Leases + Idempotency + Sagas","description":"Implement §4.19.1-§4.19.5 remote effects contract: explicit RemoteCap, named computations (no closure shipping), lease-backed liveness, idempotency keys + dedup, and saga discipline for multi-step remote workflows.

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS
- test_remote_cap_required_for_network_io: Attempt network I/O without RemoteCap in Cx; verify compile-time or runtime refusal with explicit error
- test_remote_cap_omitted_in_lab: Create LabRuntime Cx without RemoteCap; verify remote-dependent code paths fail gracefully (testable without network)
- test_named_computation_no_closure_shipping: Verify remote work is dispatched via ComputationName + serialized input bytes; verify arbitrary closures cannot be serialized/sent
- test_named_computation_registry: Register the four normative computation names (symbol_get_range, symbol_put_batch, segment_put, segment_stat); verify unregistered names are rejected
- test_lease_backed_liveness_expiry: Create a remote handle with a 100ms lease; wait 200ms without renewal; verify lease expiry triggers escalation (cancel/retry/fail) and is trace-visible
- test_idempotency_key_deterministic: Compute IdempotencyKey for same request bytes twice; verify identical keys (Trunc128(BLAKE3("fsqlite:remote:v1" || request_bytes)))
- test_idempotency_dedup_same_key_same_input: Submit same request twice with same IdempotencyKey; verify second returns recorded outcome (no double-write)
- test_idempotency_conflict_same_key_different_input: Submit two different computations with same IdempotencyKey; verify second is rejected as conflict

## E2E TEST
test_e2e_remote_effects_saga_eviction.rs: Perform an L2-to-L3 segment eviction as a Saga (upload -> verify -> retire local); cancel mid-saga after upload but before local retire; verify compensations run (uploaded segment is either cleaned up or local copy retained); restart the saga and verify it completes idempotently with no duplicate uploads.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] No network I/O occurs anywhere in FrankenSQLite without RemoteCap in the Cx capability set
- [ ] All remote operations use named computations (ComputationName enum), never serialized closures
- [ ] Lease expiry is always trace-visible and triggers deterministic escalation
- [ ] IdempotencyKey deduplication works correctly for retried requests and rejects conflicting requests
- [ ] Sagas for eviction and compaction either complete fully or compensate to a clean state","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:37:25.234174506Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T22:49:33.905311281Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T22:49:33.905289851Z","close_reason":"Enabled remote_effects module wiring in fsqlite-core and validated §4.19 RemoteCap/named-computation/idempotency/lease/saga test suite","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-numl","depends_on_id":"bd-3go","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-numl","depends_on_id":"bd-3go.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1973,"issue_id":"bd-numl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# §4.19.1 Remote Named Computations + Idempotency + Sagas

## Spec Reference
COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENSQLITE_V1.md lines 5430–5510 (§4.19, §4.19.1–§4.19.5)

## Overview
Tiered storage (L3) and replication are fundamentally remote effects. FrankenSQLite
adopts asupersync's remote contract so remote behavior is cancellable, bounded, and
auditable. All remote operations must be named (no closure shipping), idempotent,
lease-backed, and saga-wrapped for multi-step workflows.

## Global Remote Bulkhead (Normative)
All remote operations (fetch, upload, anti-entropy RPCs) MUST run under a global
remote bulkhead with concurrency cap `PRAGMA fsqlite.remote_max_in_flight`
(0 = auto; §4.17.1). Prevents retry storms and kernel-level overload on many-core
machines when remote tiers degrade.

## §4.19.1 Explicit Remote Capability (Required)
- All remote operations MUST require `RemoteCap` in `Cx`.
- Without it: no network I/O (compile-time or runtime refusal).
- Native mode still functions under `durability = local`.
- Prevents silent network I/O from arbitrary SQL code paths.
- Lab contexts simply omit RemoteCap to test without network.

## §4.19.2 Named Computations (Required for Auditing)
Remote work MUST be specified by `ComputationName` + serialized input bytes.
Runtime MUST NEVER serialize arbitrary closures.

### Normative Remote Computation Names (Minimum Set)
1. `symbol_get_range(object_id, esi_lo, esi_hi, ecs_epoch)` — fetch range of symbols
2. `symbol_put_batch(object_id, symbols[], ecs_epoch)` — upload batch of symbols
3. `segment_put(segment_id, bytes, ecs_epoch)` — upload segment blob
4. `segment_stat(segment_id, ecs_epoch)` — stat a remote segment
5. No closure shipping: all are named + serialized inputs only.

## §4.19.3 Lease-Backed Liveness (Required)
- Remote handles MUST be lease-backed.
- If lease expires: local region MUST escalate (cancel, retry, or fail).
- Event MUST be trace-visible.
- Avoids "hung remote fetch" as unbounded tail-latency failure.

## §4.19.4 Idempotency (Required)
All remote requests that might be retried MUST include an IdempotencyKey:
```
IdempotencyKey = Trunc128(BLAKE3("fsqlite:remote:v1" || request_bytes))
```
Remote receivers MUST deduplicate by IdempotencyKey (asupersync IdempotencyStore):
- Duplicate with same computation name + inputs → return recorded outcome.
- Duplicate with same key but different computation inputs → conflict, MUST reject.

## §4.19.5 Sagas for Multi-Step Publication (Required)
Any multi-step remote+local workflow that would leave partial state on
cancellation/crash MUST be expressed as a Saga (forward steps + deterministic compensations).

### 3 Normative Sagas
1. **L2→L3 eviction:** upload segment → verify remote → retire local copy.
2. **Compaction publish:** write new segments → publish → update locators/manifests.
3. **Cross-region replication:** (implied) multi-step replication workflow with
   compensation on partial failure.

Sagas are deterministic and replayable: given same inputs, same sequence of steps
and compensations occurs, evidence recorded for debugging.

## Unit Test Specifications

### T1: remote_cap_required
Attempt remote operation without RemoteCap in Cx. Verify compile-time or runtime refusal.

### T2: remote_cap_enables_network
Provide RemoteCap in Cx. Verify remote operation proceeds (mock endpoint).

### T3: named_computation_serialization
Create each of the 4 normative computation names with inputs. Verify they serialize
to ComputationName + bytes, not closures.

### T4: idempotency_key_deterministic
Compute IdempotencyKey for same request_bytes twice. Verify identical Trunc128(BLAKE3) output.

### T5: idempotency_dedup_same_inputs
Submit same request twice with same IdempotencyKey. Verify second returns recorded outcome.

### T6: idempotency_conflict_different_inputs
Submit request with same IdempotencyKey but different computation inputs.
Verify rejection (conflict error).

### T7: lease_expiry_escalation
Set up remote handle with short lease. Let lease expire. Verify escalation
(cancel/retry/fail) and trace visibility.

### T8: saga_l2_l3_eviction_forward
Execute L2→L3 eviction saga forward path: upload → verify → retire.
Verify all steps complete and evidence recorded.

### T9: saga_l2_l3_eviction_compensation
Simulate failure at verify step of L2→L3 eviction. Verify compensation
rolls back upload and local state remains intact.

### T10: saga_compaction_publish_forward
Execute compaction publish saga: write segments → publish → update locators.
Verify complete and evidence recorded.

### T11: bulkhead_concurrency_cap
Set remote_max_in_flight=2. Launch 5 concurrent remote ops. Verify at most
2 are in flight simultaneously.

### T12: saga_deterministic_replay
Replay same saga with same inputs. Verify identical step sequence.

## Dependencies
- §3.5 (RaptorQ symbols), §4.17.1 (PRAGMA remote_max_in_flight), §4.15 (governor/bulkhead),
  §7.13 (compaction), §5.6.2 (leases)
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:37:31Z"},{"id":1974,"issue_id":"bd-numl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements for §4.19.1 Remote Named Computations

### Unit Tests (fsqlite-core or fsqlite-remote crate)

**RemoteCap requirement:**
1. **test_remote_op_requires_remote_cap**: Without RemoteCap in Cx, remote operations refuse (compile-time or runtime). Verify no silent network I/O.
2. **test_lab_omits_remote_cap**: Lab contexts omit RemoteCap to test without network. Verify remote ops return appropriate error.
3. **test_native_mode_without_remote**: Under durability=local, system functions fully without RemoteCap.

**Named computations:**
4. **test_symbol_get_range_named**: `symbol_get_range(object_id, esi_lo, esi_hi, ecs_epoch)` is a named computation with serialized inputs.
5. **test_symbol_put_batch_named**: `symbol_put_batch(object_id, symbols[], ecs_epoch)` is named with serialized inputs.
6. **test_segment_put_named**: `segment_put(segment_id, bytes, ecs_epoch)` is named.
7. **test_segment_stat_named**: `segment_stat(segment_id, ecs_epoch)` is named.
8. **test_no_closure_shipping**: Verify runtime NEVER serializes arbitrary closures. Only ComputationName + serialized input bytes.

**Idempotency:**
9. **test_idempotency_key_derivation**: IdempotencyKey = Trunc128(BLAKE3("fsqlite:remote:v1" || request_bytes)). Verify correct computation.
10. **test_idempotency_dedup_same_request**: Same computation + same inputs → same IdempotencyKey → duplicate returns recorded outcome.
11. **test_idempotency_conflict_different_inputs**: Same IdempotencyKey but different computation inputs → conflict, MUST reject.
12. **test_idempotency_key_unique_per_request**: Different request bytes → different IdempotencyKeys (BLAKE3 collision resistance).

**Lease-backed liveness:**
13. **test_lease_expiry_escalates**: Remote handle lease expires → local region escalates (cancel, retry, or fail). Not a silent hang.
14. **test_lease_expiry_trace_visible**: Lease expiry event is trace-visible (appears in evidence ledger or logs).
15. **test_no_unbounded_remote_fetch**: Remote fetch that exceeds lease time is terminated. No "hung remote fetch" scenario.

**Sagas for multi-step publication:**
16. **test_saga_compensates_on_failure**: Multi-step remote+local workflow fails at step 3. Steps 1-2 are compensated (rolled back).
17. **test_saga_idempotent_compensation**: Compensation actions are themselves idempotent (safe to retry).
18. **test_saga_partial_state_impossible**: After saga completes (success or compensated failure), no partial state remains.

**Global remote bulkhead:**
19. **test_remote_bulkhead_concurrency_cap**: All remote ops run under global bulkhead. Concurrent ops capped at PRAGMA fsqlite.remote_max_in_flight.
20. **test_remote_bulkhead_zero_means_auto**: remote_max_in_flight=0 → auto-derived default.
21. **test_remote_bulkhead_prevents_retry_storms**: When remote tier degrades, bulkhead prevents runaway retry storms.

### Integration Tests
22. **test_remote_symbol_fetch_end_to_end**: Tiered storage scenario: fetch symbols from remote → decode → return page data.
23. **test_remote_saga_upload_compaction**: Multi-step: upload compacted segments → update locator → publish. Verify saga discipline.

### E2E Tests
24. **test_e2e_remote_failure_recovery**: Remote tier fails mid-operation. Verify saga compensation, bulkhead prevents cascade, local operations unaffected.

### Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: Named computation dispatch, IdempotencyKey derivation, lease renewal
- INFO: Remote operation completion (latency, computation name)
- WARN: Lease expiry, bulkhead saturation, idempotency conflict
- ERROR: Saga compensation failure (should be rare with idempotent compensators)
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:53:23Z"},{"id":1975,"issue_id":"bd-numl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Logging Augmentation (Plan Hygiene)

In addition to the existing logging requirements, ensure **all** remote-effect logs include enough structured context to debug cross-process retries deterministically:
- `trace_id` (or equivalent span/trace correlation id)
- `effect_name` / `computation_name`
- `saga_id`
- `idempotency_key`
- `attempt` (retry attempt number)
- `ecs_epoch`
- lab-only: deterministic `seed` + `schedule_fingerprint` (so failures are replayable)

This is specifically to avoid "we saw a retry storm" with no ability to reconstruct which requests were duplicates vs new work.","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:55:54Z"},{"id":1976,"issue_id":"bd-numl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented an initial §4.19.1-§4.19.5 slice:\n- Added new module crates/fsqlite-core/src/remote_effects.rs with named computation registry, RemoteCap runtime gate (plus HasRemote type bound), deterministic idempotency derivation/store, lease-backed liveness escalation, and L2->L3 eviction saga skeleton with deterministic compensation + idempotent restart behavior.\n- Added glossary constructors/accessors in crates/fsqlite-types/src/glossary.rs for RemoteCap, SymbolAuthMasterKeyCap, IdempotencyKey, Saga.\n- Exported remote_effects module from crates/fsqlite-core/src/lib.rs.\n- Added unit tests in remote_effects + glossary for idempotency, registry rejection, lease expiry escalation, bulkhead cap, and saga restart semantics.\n\nValidation status:\n- cargo check -p fsqlite-types: PASS\n- cargo test -p fsqlite-types: FAIL due pre-existing ambient-authority test failures in crates/fsqlite-harness/src/verification_gates.rs\n- cargo check --all-targets: FAIL due pre-existing errors in crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs (missing emit_* helpers)\n- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: FAIL due pre-existing fsqlite-vdbe lint error.\n\nLeaving bd-numl in_progress pending workspace stabilization / integration follow-through.","created_at":"2026-02-09T13:10:58Z"},{"id":1977,"issue_id":"bd-numl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Extended §4.19 remote-effects implementation: added PRAGMA-style remote_max_in_flight(0=>auto) executor constructor, compaction publish saga skeleton (stage->publish->locator update) with deterministic compensation/rollback semantics, and additional contract tests in crates/fsqlite-core/src/remote_effects.rs. Also hardened InMemoryRemoteStore replay behavior to reconstruct remote-visible state after compensation cleanup. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_bluemill cargo check -p fsqlite-core PASS; cargo test -p fsqlite-core remote_effects::tests:: PASS (13 tests). Workspace gates: cargo check --all-targets PASS; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings FAIL due broad pre-existing fsqlite-harness test lint debt across many files; cargo fmt --check FAIL on pre-existing formatting diffs in connection.rs and engine.rs unrelated to this slice.","created_at":"2026-02-09T13:21:42Z"}]}
